title: With All My Fondest Love
description: While hoping to reconnect with his ex-girlfriend, Noah discovers a dusty box of unread diaries and letters in his attic. Hidden inside is the forgotten story of his grandparent’s marriage. This tender true story is one grandson’s attempt to piece together their forgotten lives, from Frank Sinatra and amateur cricket to burnt letters and unspeakable loss. Noah’s family history entwines with his own messy romance, challenging him to find hope and forgiveness within the chaos of heartbreak and memory. Celebrated by the Oscar-winning writer Simon Beaufoy (The Full Monty, Slumdog Millionaire) as a 'quietly devastating, beautifully written piece of drama'. Bring your own family together for this moving and hopeful one-person play told across three generations. We'll remember those we have loved and lost and celebrate how joyful remembering them can be.
content_warnings: Discussion of still birth and cancer

reviews.excerpt: This is a memory story and its genius lies in its ability to create parallel narratives of three sets of lives, joined by family connection in a direct line of descent. Yet these parallel lives reach back through the generations while being shared alongside one another, allowing an interlacing and weaving of theme, life lessons, pain and revelation.
